enrichment.short_description: Multi-currency business account with corporate cards, FX exchange, payments, and expense tools from fintech Revolut, with plans from free to Enterprise.
enrichment.starting_price: Free entry-level plan in some markets; paid plans start around $30/month (Grow), with higher tiers up to custom Enterprise pricing
enrichment.company_size: Large (Revolut Group reports well over 10,000 employees globally; Revolut Business is one product line within the wider Revolut fintech)

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Revolut Business is the business banking arm of Revolut, the London-based fintech founded in 2015. Launched a couple of years after the consumer app, it gives companies multi-currency accounts, corporate expense cards, and payment tools through a single web dashboard and companion mobile apps, positioning itself as a fintech alternative to traditional business banking rather than a branch-based bank.

Instead of centering on lending and overdrafts the way a conventional commercial bank does, Revolut Business focuses on the mechanics of moving and managing money: holding balances in multiple currencies, issuing physical and virtual cards to employees, converting currencies at rates close to interbank pricing within plan allowances, and sending domestic and international transfers from one account.

Plans span an entry-level tier, which is free in some markets, up through Grow, Scale, and a custom Enterprise offering, with features such as bulk payments, API access, and larger fee-free FX allowances unlocked as businesses move up the tiers. The product also layers in accounting integrations, invoicing, and team permissions aimed at replacing several separate finance tools with one account.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Revolut Business Is For
paragraphs: Small and medium businesses that regularly send or receive money in more than one currency are the clearest fit, since the platform is built around multi-currency accounts and FX exchange rather than a single-currency checking account.

E-commerce sellers, agencies with overseas clients, and startups with distributed or remote teams tend to get the most value from the combination of expense cards, spend controls, and a mobile-first dashboard that lets founders or finance staff issue cards and approve payments without visiting a branch.

Companies that already run on cloud accounting software are also a good match, since Revolut Business's integrations and exportable transaction data can cut down on manual reconciliation for lean finance teams that don't have a large back-office function.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: Businesses that depend on business loans, overdrafts, or other credit facilities from their primary bank should be cautious, since Revolut Business is not built around lending in the way a traditional commercial bank is.

Cash-heavy businesses that need frequent cash deposits, or companies that require in-branch service and a dedicated relationship manager for complex treasury needs, may find a neobank model like this one a poor fit for day-to-day operations.

Companies operating in regions where Revolut Business isn't fully licensed or supported, or that need highly bespoke banking arrangements negotiated in person, are generally better served by a traditional bank or a regional business banking provider instead.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: Multi-currency accounts let businesses hold, exchange, and send funds in numerous currencies from a single account, with currency conversion priced close to interbank rates within each plan's fee-free allowance.

Expense management pairs physical and virtual company cards with spend limits, receipt capture, and approval workflows, while bulk payment tools help teams process payroll or pay multiple suppliers in one action and sync the resulting transactions with connected accounting software.

Higher-tier plans add API access for development teams building custom payment or reconciliation flows, along with more granular team permissions, reporting, and larger fee-free transfer and FX allowances for finance functions with heavier volumes.

long_form_blocks.heading: The Revolut Business Experience
paragraphs: The product is delivered through a web dashboard and companion mobile apps for iOS and Android, with most day-to-day account management, card issuance, and payment approvals handled from a phone rather than a bank branch visit.

Onboarding is largely self-serve online, and reviewers generally describe the interface as closer to a consumer fintech app than a legacy business banking portal, though some note that support quality varies and that certain issues are resolved through in-app chat rather than a dedicated phone line.

Because pricing, currency lists, and specific fee allowances vary by country, the exact day-to-day experience -including what's free versus paid- depends on which market a business signs up in, so it's worth checking the local plan page before comparing costs to a competitor.

features.title: Multi-currency accounts
features.description: Hold, exchange, and send funds in numerous currencies from a single business account instead of maintaining separate local accounts.

features.title: Corporate cards
features.description: Issue physical and virtual cards to employees with individual spending limits and controls.

features.title: Currency exchange
features.description: Convert currencies at rates close to interbank pricing within each plan's fee-free monthly allowance.

features.title: International and domestic payments
features.description: Send transfers to overseas and local accounts from the same dashboard, with allowances that scale by plan.

features.title: Bulk payments
features.description: Pay multiple suppliers or run payroll in a single batch instead of entering transfers one by one.

features.title: Expense management
features.description: Track employee spend with receipt capture, spend limits, and approval workflows tied to company cards.

features.title: Accounting integrations
features.description: Connect to accounting software to sync transaction data and cut down on manual reconciliation.

features.title: API access
features.description: Build custom payment, payout, or reconciliation workflows on higher-tier plans such as Scale and Enterprise.

features.title: Team permissions
features.description: Assign role-based access so finance staff and admins see and approve only what's relevant to their role.

features.title: Invoicing
features.description: Create and send invoices tied directly to the business account for simpler payment tracking.

pros: Multi-currency accounts and FX tools make cross-border payments simpler than with many traditional business banks.
Mobile-first app makes it easy to issue cards, approve payments, and monitor spend without visiting a branch.
Tiered plans let smaller businesses start with lower costs and add features as transaction volume grows.
Accounting integrations and built-in expense tools reduce manual reconciliation work for lean finance teams.
API access on higher plans supports custom payment and payout workflows for larger or more technical teams.
cons: Not a full-service bank: limited lending, credit, or overdraft options compared with traditional business banks.
Entry-level tier availability and exact pricing vary significantly by country, which can confuse cross-border buyers.
Customer support is primarily app- and chat-based, and some reviewers report slower responses for complex issues.
Fee-free FX and transfer allowances are capped per plan, with charges applying once an allowance is exceeded.
API access and more granular team permissions require upgrading to higher-cost tiers as usage grows.

faqs.question: Is Revolut Business free to use?
faqs.answer: It depends on the plan and country: some markets offer an entry-level tier with no monthly fee, while others start with a low-cost Basic plan, and higher tiers such as Grow, Scale, and Enterprise add a monthly subscription fee for larger transfer and FX allowances plus extra features.

faqs.question: What currencies can I hold with Revolut Business?
faqs.answer: Accounts support holding, exchanging, and sending funds in a wide range of currencies from a single account, which lets businesses manage international payments without opening separate local accounts in each currency.

faqs.question: Does Revolut Business offer physical and virtual cards?
faqs.answer: Yes, plans include physical and virtual company cards that can be issued to employees with individual spending limits and controls, with certain premium card options available on higher-tier plans.

faqs.question: Can Revolut Business connect to accounting software?
faqs.answer: Yes, the platform offers integrations designed to sync transaction data with accounting tools, which helps reduce manual reconciliation work for finance teams.

faqs.question: Does Revolut Business provide an API?
faqs.answer: API access is available on higher-tier plans such as Scale and Enterprise, letting development teams build custom payment, payout, or reconciliation workflows on top of a Revolut Business account.

use_cases.title: Cross-border e-commerce payouts
use_cases.description: Pay overseas suppliers and marketplaces in their local currency without absorbing repeated conversion fees on every transaction.

use_cases.title: Distributed team expense management
use_cases.description: Issue employee cards with individual spend limits and approval workflows for teams working across multiple countries.

use_cases.title: Multi-currency invoicing for agencies
use_cases.description: Invoice clients in their preferred currency and hold the resulting balances without converting funds on every payment received.
use_cases.audience: Agencies and freelance collectives billing international clients

use_cases.title: API-driven payouts for platforms
use_cases.description: Automate supplier, seller, or creator payouts programmatically instead of processing transfers manually one at a time.
use_cases.audience: Software platforms and marketplaces needing programmatic payouts

pricing_plans.price: Free in some markets; from £10/month in others
